content.full_text:
May 3, 1976

Ms. Natalie Roberts
Friends of the Earth
529 Commercial Street
San Francisco, CA 94111

Dear Ms. Roberts:

This letter confirms our conversation of May 3, 1976, concerning potential reporting obligations of Friends of the Earth ("FOE"). You indicated that Friends of the Earth has not yet made expenditures of $500 or more in support of Proposition 15, but anticipates making expenditures before the June 8, 1976, election. You stated that these expenditures will be made from FOE's general fund, and that FOE does not plan to carry on any specific fundraising activity to support Proposition 15. Moreover, FOE did not campaign for the Wild and Scenic Rivers Initiative, Coastal Initiative or other environmental issues that have appeared on the ballot in recent years. Consequently, dues received by FOE are not "contributions." Thus, FOE is required to file a statement of organization because the organization will not receive contributions totaling $500 or more to support Proposition 15. Government Code Sections 84101, 82013(a).

Friends of the Earth publishes a bimonthly newsletter, Not Man Apart, and plans to devote an entire issue of the newsletter to Proposition 15. The costs of the special issue are not expenditures. However, if additional costs are incurred because the issue devoted to Proposition 15 is printed on other than its regular schedule, expanded in circulation or substantially altered in style, size or format, such additional costs are expenditures. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225(c)(4)(C).

Costs incurred by Friends of the Earth are expenditures if the payments are used for communications which expressly advocate the passage of a clearly identified ballot measure. If FOE pays the expenses of a person to attend a meeting of the steering committee of Californians for Nuclear Safeguards, I recommend that you report these costs as expenditures because they are directly incidental to communications in support of Proposition 15.

Ms. Natalie Roberts
May 3, 1976
Page Two


     Lastly, the treasurer of the national organization is
not required to sign campaign statements. Any responsible
person can serve as the treasurer of the campaign committee.
See Opinion requested by John H. Augustine 1 FPPC Ops. 69,
No. 75-064, July 2, 1975), enclosed.

     Copies of regulations defining "contribution" and "expen-
diture" are enclosed, along with a fact sheet setting forth
the filing schedule for committees supporting or opposing
ballot measures. I hope that this information will be of
assistance.

                                        Sincerely,




                                        Natalie E. West
                                        Staff Counsel

NEN:plh

Enclosures: 1 FPPC Ops. 69
            Reg. 18215
            Reg. 18225
            Yellow Fact Sheet

sections.conclusion: FOE is NOT required to file a statement of organization because the organization will not receive contributions totaling $500 or more to support Proposition 15. Additional costs for newsletter issues devoted to Proposition 15 are expenditures only if incurred due to printing schedule changes, circulation expansion, or format alterations. Costs for communications expressly advocating passage of Proposition 15 are expenditures, including costs for persons attending steering committee meetings. The national organization treasurer is not required to sign campaign statements.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting and disclosure obligations for Friends of the Earth regarding potential expenditures in support of Proposition 15, including questions about contribution requirements, newsletter costs, and treasurer signing authority?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Friends of the Earth is not required to file a statement of organization as it will not receive contributions totaling $500 or more for Proposition 15. Additional newsletter costs attributable to the Proposition 15 issue are expenditures only if they exceed normal costs due to schedule, circulation, or format changes. Any responsible person can serve as campaign treasurer, not necessarily the national organization's treasurer.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Advice letter responding to telephonic conversation; no formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers, but clear question-and-answer structure evident; document includes specific regulatory citations and enclosed enclosures; some marginal notation (NEW:plh) suggests internal processing.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the reporting and disclosure obligations for Friends of the Earth regarding potential expenditures in support of Proposition 15, including questions about contribution requirements, newsletter costs, and treasurer signing authority?

CONCLUSION: FOE is NOT required to file a statement of organization because the organization will not receive contributions totaling $500 or more to support Proposition 15. Additional costs for newsletter issues devoted to Proposition 15 are expenditures only if incurred due to printing schedule changes, circulation expansion, or format alterations. Costs for communications expressly advocating passage of Proposition 15 are expenditures, including costs for persons attending steering committee meetings. The national organization treasurer is not required to sign campaign statements.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Friends of the Earth on reporting obligations for Proposition 15 expenditures, clarifying that no statement of organization is required, specifying when newsletter costs constitute reportable expenditures, and confirming that any responsible person may serve as campaign treasurer.
